title: Quiz: QPE Analysis
description: Test your understanding of QPE output analysis and probability bounds

questions.question: If φ = 5/16 and we use t=4 counting qubits, QPE will measure |5⟩ with probability:
questions.explanation: φ = 5/16 = 5/2^4 is an exact phase with 4 qubits, so QPE returns |5⟩ = |0101⟩ with certainty.

questions.question: The minimum guaranteed success probability (within ±1 of optimal) for QPE is approximately:
To achieve 8-bit precision with 99% success probability (ε=0.01), we need approximately:
In Shor's algorithm, continued fractions are used to:

options.text: Extract the period r from QPE measurement

questions.explanation: QPE gives m/2^t ≈ s/r. Continued fractions find r from this approximation.

questions.question: Adding more counting qubits always increases QPE success probability.
questions.explanation: More qubits mean finer grid resolution and higher probability of landing near the true phase.
